response.justification: Strong geospatial domain expertise through GISCollective and mapping applications, plus solid distributed systems and quality culture background, but significant concern about C++ recency—last professional C++ work ended 10+ years ago with no Rust experience. Interviewing could clarify learning velocity on modern C++ and whether recent systems-level programming depth exists beneath the web/backend focus of recent roles.
strengths: Deep geospatial domain expertise and production experience building mapping/GIS applications through GISCollective (open-source) and Green Map projects
Proven distributed systems knowledge from Dunnhumby's microservices handling millions of requests/second and real-time data processing with caching and performance optimization
Consistent emphasis on testing rigor, software craftsmanship, and quality—authored Fluent-Asserts and Trial test framework, and actively teach TDD methodology
concerns: C++ experience ended December 2014; over 10 years without production modern C++ work, and zero Rust experience despite both being core to the role
Primarily web and Node.js/TypeScript focused in recent roles; limited recent systems-level programming beyond DLang work that concluded ~2020
No demonstrated production experience optimizing or operating critical routing data pipelines, tile generation, or geospatial data freshness systems at scale

key_factors.factor: Distributed systems and quality engineering background
